candidate_problems.text: How can the proposed notion of a 'relevant criticism set' be specified non-circularly, so that hard-to-vary does not just mean 'resists the criticisms we happened to choose'?
Can there be explanations that are genuinely hard to vary because of dense internal constraints yet still false, and if so what additional criterion separates robustly false systems from knowledge?
How should load-bearing parts be identified in explanations that admit multiple equivalent formulations, where local wording changes do not matter but deeper structural substitutions do?
What algorithm could systematically search for criticism-preserving transformations without collapsing into brute-force variation over representations rather than explanatory structure?
